docs.title: PowerPoint Components
docs.text: Build presentation slides with reusable Python components and a builder-style layout flow.

docs.title: Why this docs site
docs.text:
• Component-level API reference generated directly from code.
  • Searchable docs for quick discovery.
  • Practical examples tuned for presentation design workflows.

docs.title: Start Here
docs.text:
• Browse Text Box for text-focused components.
  • Browse Chart for chart and data-visualization APIs.
  • Use Component Reference for a broad manual lookup.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
python -m venv .venv
.venv\Scripts\activate
python -m pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Local Docs Preview
docs.text:
mkdocs serve

Open http://127.0.0.1:8000

docs.title: Components Documentation
docs.text: This folder contains GitHub-friendly docs for all presentation components in this repository.

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text:
• Component Reference
  • Composition Patterns

docs.title: Public vs Internal Imports
docs.text:
Most components are available from top-level pptx_components imports:

import pptx_components as pc

A few components currently exist in the codebase but are not re-exported at the package root. Import those from module paths directly:

from pptx_components.components.timeline import Timeline
from pptx_components.components.comparison import ComparisonPanel

docs.title: Minimal Slide Pattern
docs.text:
from pptx import Presentation
import pptx_components as pc

prs = Presentation()
pc.set_theme(pc.DarkTheme())

b = pc.SlideBuilder(prs)
b.add(pc.SectionHeader("Status"))
b.skip(0.1)
b.add_row(
    pc.MetricCard("Revenue", "$1.2M", delta="+18%", delta_positive=True),
    pc.MetricCard("Users", "24,370", delta="+7%", delta_positive=True),
    pc.MetricCard("Churn", "3.2%", delta="+0.4pp", delta_positive=False),
    h=1.5,
)

prs.save("example.pptx")

docs.title: Composition Patterns
docs.text: This page shows practical patterns for composing slides with SlideBuilder.

docs.title: 1) Executive Dashboard Pattern
docs.text:
import pptx_components as pc

b.add(pc.TitleBlock("Executive Dashboard", "Weekly operating metrics"))
b.skip(0.1)
b.add_row(
    pc.MetricCard("MRR", "$128K", delta="+12%", delta_positive=True),
    pc.MetricCard("NPS", "72", delta="+4pts", delta_positive=True),
    pc.MetricCard("Churn", "3.2%", delta="+0.4pp", delta_positive=False),
    h=1.5,
)
b.skip(0.15)
b.add(pc.BarChart(["Jan", "Feb", "Mar"], {"Revenue": [90, 98, 105]}), h=2.4)

docs.title: 2) Status And Risk Pattern
docs.text:
b.add(pc.SectionHeader("Operational Status", badge_text="WEEK 24"))
b.skip(0.1)
b.add(pc.CalloutBox("Payment retry latency above target in EU region.", style="warning"))
b.add(pc.CalloutBox("API error rate stabilized after hotfix.", style="success"))
b.skip(0.1)
b.add(pc.ProgressBar("API v3 rollout", 62))
b.add(pc.ProgressBar("Infrastructure migration", 40))

docs.title: 3) Product Story Pattern With Navigation
docs.text:
b.add(
    pc.TabsPanel(
        ["Overview", "Analytics", "Risks", "Decisions"],
        active_index=1,
        title="Q2 Review",
        variant="line",
        content="Activation improved by 6% and CAC dropped 8%.",
    ),
    h=1.9,
)
b.skip(0.15)
b.add(
    pc.StepFlow(
        ["Discovery", "Prototype", "Validation", "Launch", "Scale"],
        current=2,
        statuses=["done", "done", "current", "pending", "pending"],
    ),
    h=1.4,
)

docs.title: 4) Data + Legend + KPI Grid Pattern
docs.text:
legend_items = [
    ("APAC", (59, 130, 246)),
    ("EMEA", (16, 185, 129)),
    ("Americas", (249, 115, 22)),
]

b.add_row(
    pc.ImageBlock("examples/demo_dark_slides/slide_004.png", mode="contain"),
    pc.Legend(legend_items, title="Region Color Legend"),
    h=2.5,
    weights=[1.6, 1.0],
)
b.skip(0.1)
b.add(
    pc.KPIGrid(
        [
            ("New Leads", "1,284", "+9%", True),
            ("Conversion", "14.2%", "+1.1pp", True),
            ("Win Rate", "31%", "-2pp", False),
            ("Sales Cycle", "27 days", "-3 days", True),
        ],
        cols=2,
    ),
    h=2.2,
)

docs.title: 5) Internal Components Pattern
docs.text:
If you want to use components not yet re-exported at package root:

from pptx_components.components.timeline import Timeline
from pptx_components.components.comparison import ComparisonPanel

b.add(
    Timeline(
        [
            ("Jan", "Discovery", "done"),
            ("Feb", "Prototype", "done"),
            ("Mar", "Validation", "current"),
            ("Apr", "Launch", "upcoming"),
        ],
        title="Roadmap",
    ),
    h=1.8,
)

b.add(
    ComparisonPanel(
        "Before",
        ["Manual reporting", "Siloed dashboards", "Late alerts"],
        "After",
        ["Automated pipeline", "Unified metrics", "Near real-time alerts"],
        title="Transformation",
    ),
    h=2.2,
)

docs.title: Component Reference
docs.text: This reference documents constructor parameters and common usage snippets.

docs.title: Precedence
docs.text:
Theme resolution follows this order:

  1. Explicit component-level theme passed to render(..., theme=...).
  2. Local section override applied by Container(local_theme=..., theme_patch=...).
  3. SlideBuilder(theme=...) scope.
  4. Global fallback from set_theme(...) / get_theme().

docs.title: Container Section Override API
docs.text:
Import: pc.Container

Signature additions:

Container(
    child: Component,
    padding: float | None = None,
    fill_rgb: tuple[int, int, int] | None = None,
    border_rgb: tuple[int, int, int] | None = None,
    radius: float = 0.05,
    local_theme: Theme | None = None,
    theme_patch: Mapping[str, object] | None = None,
)

theme_patch lets you patch only selected theme tokens for a section subtree. Supported keys include spacing/typography constants and semantic color tokens:

  • DISPLAY, HEADING, SUBHEADING, BODY, CAPTION
  • XS, SM, MD, LG, XL
  • SLIDE_W, SLIDE_H, MARGIN
  • BG, SURFACE, SURFACE_ALT
  • TEXT_PRIMARY, TEXT_SECONDARY, TEXT_MUTED
  • ACCENT, ACCENT_SOFT, CALLOUT, POSITIVE, NEGATIVE

Example:

section = pc.Container(
    pc.Column(
        pc.MetricCard("Pipeline", "$3.4M", "+6%", True),
        pc.TextCard("Section note", "This block uses a warmer local surface."),
    ),
    theme_patch={
        "SURFACE": (255, 248, 236),
        "SURFACE_ALT": (246, 230, 206),
        "TEXT_PRIMARY": (68, 44, 22),
        "MD": 0.18,
    },
)

docs.title: React Mental Model Mapping
docs.text:
• SlideBuilder(theme=...) is analogous to ThemeProvider scope.
  • Container(local_theme=..., theme_patch=...) is analogous to nested provider overrides.
  • render(..., theme=...) is analogous to per-component explicit prop override.
  • set_theme(...) is analogous to process-level default context value.

docs.title: DataTable
docs.text:
Import: pc.DataTable

Signature:

DataTable(
    headers: list[str],
    rows: list[list[str]],
    weights: list[float] | None = None,
    zebra: bool = True,
    accent_header: bool = True,
)

Notes:

  • weights must match header count.
  • every row length must match header count.

docs.title: BarChart
docs.text:
Import: pc.BarChart

Signature:

BarChart(
    categories: list[str],
    series: dict[str, list[float]],
    title: str | None = None,
    stacked: bool = False,
    mode: str | None = None,
)

Supported mode values:

  • column_clustered
  • column_stacked
  • column_stacked_100
  • bar_clustered
  • bar_stacked

docs.title: Chart
docs.text: Data-visualization components for bars, lines, pie, and scatter patterns.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.chart.BarChart
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Vertical bar chart.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default categories list[str]

X-axis labels.

required series dict[str, list[float]]

Dict of series name → list of values.

required title str | None

Optional chart title.

None stacked bool

Use stacked bar chart. Deprecated — use mode= instead.

False mode str | None

Optional explicit chart mode. Supported values: "column_clustered" (default), "column_stacked", "column_stacked_100", "bar_clustered", "bar_stacked".

None

docs.title: pptx_components.components.chart.ScatterChart
docs.text:
Bases: Component

XY scatter chart for correlation analysis and portfolio views.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default series dict[str, list[tuple[float, float]]]

Dict of series name -> list of (x, y) tuples.

required title str | None

Optional chart title.

None x_label str | None

Optional label for the x-axis.

None y_label str | None

Optional label for the y-axis.

None

docs.title: pptx_components.components.scatter.ScatterPlot
docs.text:
Bases: Component

XY scatter plot visualization for correlation and distribution analysis.

Renders data points as circles on a 2D grid. Useful for: - Correlation analysis (feature vs. performance) - Outlier detection - Risk/return quadrants - Portfolio positioning

Axes
  • X-axis: Horizontal numeric range
  • Y-axis: Vertical numeric range
  • Optional quadrant dividers and labels
  • Configurable axis labels and tick marks
Data Points
  • Each point: (x, y, size=0.15, color=theme.ACCENT)
  • Optional point labels (text annotation)
  • Optional grouping by color

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default points list[tuple[float, float, str | None, tuple[int, int, int] | None, float]]

List of (x, y, label=None, color_rgb=None, size=0.15) tuples. x, y: Numeric coordinates (will be scaled to grid). label: Optional text label for point. color_rgb: Optional (R, G, B) point color; theme.ACCENT used if None. size: Point radius in inches. Default: 0.15 (small dot).

required x_label str | None

X-axis label string (e.g. "Features", "Cost").

None y_label str | None

Y-axis label string (e.g. "Performance", "Value").

None title str | None

Optional plot title.

None x_range tuple[float, float] | None

(min, max) numeric range for x-axis. Auto-calculated if None.

None y_range tuple[float, float] | None

(min, max) numeric range for y-axis. Auto-calculated if None.

None show_grid bool

Whether to draw gridlines. Default: True.

True quadrant_labels tuple[str, str, str, str] | None

(top_left, top_right, bottom_left, bottom_right) text labels for quadrants.

None Example

scatter = ScatterPlot( ... points=[ ... (2.5, 8.0, "Algorithm A", (100, 200, 255), 0.2), ... (5.0, 6.5, "Algorithm B", (100, 200, 255), 0.2), ... (3.0, 4.0, "Outlier", (255, 100, 100), 0.25), ... ], ... x_label="Complexity", ... y_label="Accuracy (%)", ... title="Model Performance Landscape", ... quadrant_labels=("High Perf", "Ideal", "Low Complexity", "Avoid") ... )

docs.title: pptx_components.components.waterfall.WaterfallChart
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Cumulative delta (waterfall) chart built from themed rectangles.

Each bar floats at the cumulative level, showing a positive (POSITIVE color) or negative (NEGATIVE color) contribution. An optional Total bar spans from zero to the final sum and is rendered in ACCENT color.

No external chart library required — uses pptx rectangles directly.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default categories list[str]

Label for each delta step.

required values list[float]

Positive or negative change at each step.

required title str | None

Optional heading above the chart.

None show_total bool

Append a summary "Total" bar.

True total_label str

Label for the total bar.

'Total'

docs.title: Components Guide
docs.text: This section contains practical notes for using the component system effectively.

docs.title: Public vs Internal Imports
docs.text:
Most components are available from top-level imports:

import pptx_components as pc

Some components may be imported directly from module paths when not re-exported:

from pptx_components.components.timeline import Timeline
from pptx_components.components.comparison import ComparisonPanel

docs.title: Minimal Slide Pattern
docs.text:
from pptx import Presentation
import pptx_components as pc

prs = Presentation()
pc.set_theme(pc.DarkTheme())

builder = pc.SlideBuilder(prs)
builder.add(pc.SectionHeader("Status"))
builder.skip(0.1)
builder.add_row(
    pc.MetricCard("Revenue", "$1.2M", delta="+18%", delta_positive=True),
    pc.MetricCard("Users", "24,370", delta="+7%", delta_positive=True),
    pc.MetricCard("Churn", "3.2%", delta="+0.4pp", delta_positive=False),
    h=1.5,
)

prs.save("example.pptx")

docs.title: Text Box
docs.text: Text-focused components for narrative surfaces, headers, and structured copy.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.text_card.TextCard
docs.text:
Bases: Component

General-purpose surface card for narrative text and body copy.

A flexible rectangular text panel with an optional title, left accent bar, and rounded corners. Three styles control the surface and text palette.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default body str

The main body copy to display.

required title str | None

Optional heading rendered above the body in bold.

None style str

"default" | "muted" | "accent"

'default'

docs.title: pptx_components.components.title.TitleBlock
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Full-width title with optional subtitle and top accent bar.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.title.SectionHeader
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Horizontal section break with left accent bar and optional right-aligned badge.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.narrative.NarrativePage
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Framed long-form narrative panel with page metadata and key takeaways.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.narrative.NarrativeTwoColumnPage
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Long-form narrative layout with a right-side evidence or takeaway rail.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.narrative.LongNarrativeBlock
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Auto-paginated narrative component for long-form slide storytelling.

Use paginate_narrative(...) if you want to pre-generate all pages, or pass page to render one page at a time.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.narrative.paginate_narrative
docs.text:
paginate_narrative(
    text: str,
    max_chars_per_page: int = 700,
    max_paragraphs_per_page: int = 3,
) -> list[str]

Split long narrative text into slide-friendly pages.

Paragraphs are preserved when possible and oversized paragraphs are sentence-split before word-level fallback.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.narrative.build_narrative_slides
docs.text:
build_narrative_slides(
    prs,
    title: str,
    text: str,
    summary: str | None = None,
    key_points: list[str] | None = None,
    sidebar_title: str | None = None,
    sidebar_points: list[str] | None = None,
    sidebar_note: str | None = None,
    max_chars_per_page: int = 700,
    max_paragraphs_per_page: int = 3,
    theme: Theme | None = None,
) -> list

Generate one slide per narrative page and return created slide objects.
